channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump tariffs pose danger for vulnerable Republicans
item.description: Vulnerable House Republicans are being put on defense amid the fallout from President Donald Trump’s sweeping global tariffs, which have stoked uncertainty about the economy.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: In conservative Alabama, Republicans are cheering for Trump - with some quiet concerns and caveats
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (AP) — On a day when stock markets around the world dropped precipitously, Alabama Republican Party Chairman John Wahl led a celebration of the president whose global tariffs sparked the selloff.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rescue efforts from Myanmar's deadly earthquake wind down as death toll exceeds 3,500
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Long-shot efforts to find survivors from Myanmar’s devastating March 28 earthquake were winding down Monday, as rescue efforts were supplanted by increasing relief and recovery activity, with the death toll from the disaster surpassing 3,500 and still climbing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump says he's not backing down, calls tariffs 'medicine' as markets reel
item.description: Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, Trump said he didn't want global markets to fall, but also that he wasn't concerned about the massive sell-off either.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: George M. Johnson's 'All Boys Aren't Blue' tops 2024 list of most 'challenged' US library books
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Removing books from library shelves is no longer just a story of objections from a local community or an individual parent, the American Library Association says.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here's what to know about nations considering the 1st global tax on emissions for shipping
item.description: Nations are trying to reach an agreement to charge commercial vessels a fee for their emissions in what would effectively be the world’s first global carbon tax.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missing California mom’s family digs for homicide ‘cover-up or cleanup’ as police eye persons of interest
item.description: The 2024 disappearance of a California mom of four is now being investigated as a homicide as her sisters strive to find answers in the puzzling case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Northern Border 'quiet crisis' brews as expert floats unconventional solution to combat human smuggling
item.description: As a 'quiet crisis' unfolds along the U.S.-Canada border, one expert advocates for the harnessing of cutting-edge technologies like artificial intelligence to combat human smuggling.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New bombshell study reveals 'assassination culture' spreading on the left under President Trump
item.description: More than half of left-leaning Americans say it would be at least “somewhat justified" to assassinate Donald Trump, according to a new study shared exclusively with Fox News Digital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Swollen rivers flood towns in US South after dayslong deluge of rain
item.description: FRANKFORT, Kentucky (AP) — Days of unrelenting heavy rain and storms that killed at least 18 people worsened flooding as some rivers rose to near-record levels and inundated towns across an already saturated U.S. South and parts of the Midwest.
item.dc:creator: JON CHERRY, KIMBERLEE KRUESI and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Former US Rep. Mia Love honored in Utah after brain cancer death
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Family and friends of former U.S. Rep. Mia Love are set to gather Monday in Salt Lake City to honor her life and legacy after she died of brain cancer in March at age 49. Love, a daughter of Haitian immigrants, was the first Black Republican woman elected to Congress.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Minivan plows through crowd injuring at least 9 in busy Los Angeles shopping district
item.description: A minivan drove into a crowd in Downtown Los Angeles on Sunday, injuring at least nine people, including three children. No fatalities were reported.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: RFK Jr. visits epicenter of Texas measles outbreak after death of second child who was infected
item.description: U.S. Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. visited the epicenter of Texas' still-growing measles outbreak on Sunday, the same day a funeral was held for a second young child who was not vaccinated and died from a measles-related illness.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI and AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Carroll County residents ordered to evacuate as water level rises: Here's what we know
item.description: "All we know for sure is that this is going to impact our area in a major way," Judge Executive Humphrey wrote in the evacuation order.
item.dc:creator: Corey Elam, NewsNation

item.title: Pres. Nelson announces 15 new temples during April 2025 general conference
item.description: President Russell M. Nelson of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints announced several new temples during the church's April 2025 general conference.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Houston, Florida play for title and put a different spin on the underdog role to wrap March Madness
item.description: Coach Kelvin Sampson’s squad of defenders and deniers face Florida for the national title Monday night. They wrap up a front-runner’s Final Four that featured all No. 1 seeds but ends with the two top ones — Auburn and Duke — sitting at home.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump economic adviser defends tariffs: Not a 'big effect' on US consumer
item.description: National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett defended President Trump's sweeping global tariffs that have stoked concerns about the United States economy.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Scully, NewsNation

item.title: Second child died from measles-related causes in West Texas
item.description: A second school-aged child in West Texas has died from a measles-related illness, a hospital spokesman confirmed Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida charter captain arrested after viral altercation with young fisherman
item.description: Police in Punta Gorda spoke out about the incident Saturday, saying "this type of behavior will not be tolerated.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DOJ lawyer who criticized admin over Maryland man's deportation placed on leave
item.description: Erez Reuveni, one of the DOJ's senior immigration lawyers, has been placed on leave after criticizing the Trump administration over mistakenly deporting a Maryland man to a prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stellantis joins Ford in offering employee discounts to public
item.description: The move follows in the steps of Ford Motor Company, which announced a similar bargain earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Protesters from New York to Alaska assail Trump and Musk in 'Hands Off!' rallies
item.description: Crowds of people angry about the way President Donald Trump is running the country marched and rallied in scores of American cities Saturday in the biggest day of demonstrations yet by an opposition movement trying to regain its momentum after the shock of the Republican’s first weeks in office.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Poor air quality increases depression risk, study finds
item.description: A new study finds poor air quality is linked to a heightened risk for depression. Depression is a classified as a mood disorder.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, KRON4

item.title: At least 16 dead in flooding, tornadoes as storms slash parts of US
item.description: Many of the impacted areas already are heavily waterlogged by days of severe storms that spawned deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the severe storms and flash flooding hitting parts of the US
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Parts of the South and Midwest, still reeling from violent storms, tornadoes and flooding that have killed more than a dozen people, face new flooding and tornado warnings that forecasters said could last for days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Houston rallies to beat Duke 70-67 in the Final Four, will face Florida for the NCAA title
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Sorry guys, “Phi Slama Jama” is already taken. How ’bout something simple, like “Comeback Kids.” “National champs” might be a possibility, too.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: California culinary star dies after hiking in Hawaii
item.description: Gianna Buzzetta was killed in a freak accident in Kauai, Hawaii, while hiking with her boyfriend last week.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, NewsNation

item.title: Storms tore up two of America's most iconic trails. Federal cuts have disrupted repairs
item.description: CAMPO, Calif. (AP) — Hiking the Pacific Crest Trail is a challenge, especially for adventurers making the entire run from Southern California to Canada, and Eric Kipperman’s job is to greet them at the start and lay bare the difficulties ahead.
item.dc:creator: JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Clayton leads Florida to NCAA title game, scoring 34 points in 79-73 victory over SEC rival Auburn
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Walter Clayton Jr. is having an NCAA Tournament for the ages. Florida now just needs him to deliver one more time.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Several University of Massachusetts international students have visas revoked
item.description: Five international students at the University of Massachusetts Amherst are the latest in the country to have their student visas revoked by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Angry protesters from New York to Alaska assail Trump and Musk in 'Hands Off!' rallies
item.description: Crowds of people angry about the way President Donald Trump is running the country marched and rallied in scores of American cities Saturday in the biggest day of demonstrations yet by an opposition movement trying to regain its momentum after the shock of the Republican’s first weeks in office.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Justice Department places attorney who struggled to explain Maryland man's deportation on leave
item.description: A Justice Department attorney who struggled in court on Friday to explain the Trump administration's deportation of a Maryland man to El Salvador has been placed on administrative leave, a department official said.
item.dc:creator: By Ken Dilanian, Gary Grumbach and Raquel Coronell Uribe, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump goes all in with bet that the heavy price of tariffs will pay off for Americans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Not even 24 hours after his party lost a key Wisconsin race and underperformed in Florida, President Donald Trump followed the playbook that has defined his political career: He doubled down. Trump’s move Wednesday to place stiff new tariffs on imports from nearly all U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Protesters rally against Trump, Musk in 'Hands Off!' gatherings nationwide
item.description: Thousands of protesters gathered nationwide Saturday to protest Trump administration policies, including federal downsizing, reciprocal tariffs and immigration reform.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turkish student detained by ICE moved to Vermont before judge's order, government says
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Tufts University doctoral student from Turkey who was detained by immigration authorities had been moved to Vermont by the time a federal judge ordered authorities to keep her in Massachusetts, lawyers for the U.S. government said.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4
